html:
Dragonpie is a Fruit Food item cooked in the Crock Pot by using 1 Dragon Fruit and other fillers. It takes 40 seconds to cook.

Adding any Meats will result in Meatballs or other meat based recipes. Adding a Mandrake will result in Mandrake Soup. Adding 3 Sweeteners will result in Taffy. Adding more than 1 Durian will result in Monster Lasagna unless Twigs are also added. Adding an Eggplant and another Vegetable may result in Stuffed Eggplant. Adding Banana could result in a Banana Shake.

As it is not a meat food item, Wigfrid will not eat it.


Downloadable Content

In all DLCs, Dragonpie increases the player's perceived temperature by 1 degree per second for 10 seconds when consumed.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, adding Banana, Ice, and Twig as fillers will result in Banana Pop instead.

In the Hamlet DLC, Dragonpie can be bought for 30 Oincs at The Sterling Trough Deli, making it the most expensive food from the shop.

Prerequisites

  • Requires:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:

Recipe

×3

Cannot include Meats or Mandrakes

Examples

×3

 A recipe for Dragon Pie that drastically extenuates hunger value.

×3

×2

×2

50% chance to make Stuffed Eggplant

×3

×3

Usage

Tips

  • This food item can heal much more Health and Hunger than most of its ingredients. Due to this, and the rarity of Dragon Fruits, it is best to cook Dragonpies rather than eating Dragon Fruits on their own.
  • In the Hamlet DLC, it is best to completely avoid buying Dragonpie from The Sterling Trough Deli, as it is nearly twice as expensive as meals such as Honey Ham which have similar stats.
  • Because Wurt will not eat meat, Dragonpies are a fairly decent substitute to Honey Ham, Beefy Greens and Bacon and Eggs, as they have a fairly identical statline of large hunger and decent healing. Dragonpie also boasts an impressive 15-day spoilage time, which can be considerably useful.

Trivia

  • During the Pig Fiesta event in Hamlet, Dragonpie takes on a more festive appearance.

Gallery

  • Dragonpie in the Winter's Feast update poster.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord

–Wurt
“Is it made from actual dragons?”
–Walter
“When in doubt, bake it into a pie.”
–Wanda

Tóm tắt

Bánh Thanh Long là một Thực Phẩm Trái Cây được nấu trong Nồi Hầm bằng cách sử dụng 1 Thanh Long và các chất bổ sung khác. Phải mất 40 giây để nấu ăn.

'''Dragonpie''' is a [[Fruit]] [[Food]] [[item]] cooked in the [[Crock Pot]] by using 1 [[Dragon Fruit]] and other fillers. It takes 40 seconds to cook.

Adding any [[Meats]] will result in [[Meatballs]] or other meat based recipes. Adding a [[Mandrake]] will result in [[Mandrake Soup]]. Adding 3 [[Sweetener|Sweeteners]] will result in [[Taffy]]. Adding more than 1 [[Durian]] will result in [[Monster Lasagna]] unless [[Twigs]] are also added. Adding an [[Eggplant]] and another [[Vegetable]] may result in [[Stuffed Eggplant]]. Adding [[Banana]] could result in a [[Banana Shake]].

As it is not a meat food item, [[Wigfrid]] will not eat it.

__NOTOC__
== {{Pic|32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
In all [[DLC]]s, Dragonpie increases the player's perceived temperature by 1 degree per second for 10 seconds when consumed.

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, adding [[Banana]], [[Ice]], and Twig as fillers will result in [[Banana Pop]] instead.

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Dragonpie can be bought for 30 [[Oinc]]s at [[Pig Shops|The Sterling Trough Deli]], making it the most expensive food from the shop.

== Prerequisites ==
* Requires: [[File:Dragon Fruits.png|link=Dragon Fruit]]
* Excludes: [[File:Meats Crock Pot.png|link=Meats]]
* Excludes: {{Pic|64|Mandrake}}
* Excludes: [[File:Asparagus.png|link=Asparagus]]

== {{Pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Dragon Fruit
|item2 = Filler
|count2 = 3
|result = Dragon Pie
|tool = Crock Pot
|note = Cannot include [[Meats]] or [[Mandrake]]s
}}

== Examples ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Dragon Fruits
|item2 = Twigs
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Dragon Pie
|note={{Pic24|Prototype}} A recipe for Dragon Pie that drastically extenuates hunger value.}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Dragon Fruits
|item2 = Berry
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Dragon Pie
}}

|count2 = 2
|item3 = Twigs
|result = Dragon Pie
|tool = Crock Pot
}}

{{Recipe

|item2 = Eggplants
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Berry
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Dragon Pie
|note = 50% chance to make [[Stuffed Eggplant]]
}}

{{Recipe

|item2 = Ice
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Dragon Pie
|dlc = Reign of Giants
}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Dragon Fruits
|item2 = Weevole Carapace
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Dragon Pie
|dlc = Hamlet
}}

== {{Pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage ==
{{Recipe
|item = Dragonpie
|tool = Pig Icon
|result = Manure
}}

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* This food item can heal much more [[Health]] and [[Hunger]] than most of its ingredients. Due to this, and the rarity of Dragon Fruits, it is best to cook Dragonpies rather than eating Dragon Fruits on their own.
* In the ''Hamlet'' DLC, it is best to completely avoid buying Dragonpie from [[The Sterling Trough Deli]], as it is nearly twice as expensive as meals such as [[Honey Ham]] which have similar stats.
*Because [[Wurt]] will not eat meat, Dragonpies are a fairly decent substitute to [[Honey Ham]], [[Beefy Greens]] and [[Bacon and Eggs]], as they have a fairly identical statline of large hunger and decent healing. Dragonpie also boasts an impressive 15-day spoilage time, which can be considerably useful.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
[[File:Dragonpie (Pig Fiesta).png|right]]
* During the [[Pig Fiesta]] event in ''Hamlet'', Dragonpie takes on a more festive appearance.
